findings: Verified as an adult male Rose-breasted Grosbeak: correct black head/back, pale heavy triangular bill, white underparts and wing/tail patches, and rose-red breast chevron. The illustration has one head, one beak, two legs, and one tail; the far wing is naturally occluded, while the lower wing is a separate anatomical study.

All visible text is factually consistent with the verification sources, including family, scientific name, behavior, habitat, and measurements. No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears.

Minor visual stylization: the breast patch is broader and more vertically extended than in the supplied photos, and the tail is rendered a little less square-ended; neither is a material species-identification error.

verification_sources.title: Rose-breasted Grosbeak Identification — All About Birds
